methodology: Refactored to Empowered-style OKRs: a small set of company objectives, linked source objectives, source-level key results, explicit team ownership, and limited use of High Integrity Commitments.
okrDesignPrinciples: Objectives describe meaningful business and customer outcomes, not feature delivery.
Key results measure source objectives, while source objectives map upward into a small set of company objectives.
Every key result names executing and supporting teams so accountability is explicit.
High Integrity Commitments are used sparingly and only for work that is materially deadline-sensitive.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q1 Make execution reliably repeatable
companyObjectives.statement: Improve the operating system behind the product so launches, service recovery, reliability, and cross-functional delivery become predictably repeatable.
companyObjectives.okrDesignNotes: Keeps teams accountable for operational outcomes and avoids turning roadmap delivery into a checklist of feature shipments.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q1 Turn product progress into economic leverage
companyObjectives.statement: Translate better customer and operational outcomes into stronger revenue quality, retention, and strategic leverage for the business.
companyObjectives.okrDesignNotes: Separates commercial results from activity metrics so leaders can evaluate whether product work is compounding into business value.

objectives.title: 2026-Q1 Make employer bike-program launches repeatable
objectives.objective: Reduce employer rollout friction so new programs go live faster, convert more eligible employees into riders, and create a stronger renewal case within the first quarter.

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Share of eligible employees who activate and keep using a company bike program.
Time from contract signature to employee enrollment readiness.

linkedBusinessKPIs.kpiName: Net revenue retention from employer programs
linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Revenue retention and expansion across employer accounts after churn, contraction, and expansion.

keyResults.target: Cut launch time for newly signed employers
keyResults.baseline: Current rollout duration varies by market and integration complexity
keyResults.statement: Reduce the time from employer signature to enrollment-ready launch by standardizing policy setup and payroll onboarding.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to reduce the time from employer signature to enrollment-ready launch by standardizing policy setup and payroll onboarding via Eligibility, Payroll & HRIS Sync and Enterprise Integration Layer.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for program go-live lead time, aligned with its focus on own eventing, workflow, case, and communication primitives reused across launches, claims, and lifecycle operations via Eventing & Process Backbone and Workflow, Case & Exception Management.

Support the KR by contributing capability for program go-live lead time, aligned with its focus on own observability, runtime reliability, security controls, and identity foundations for the bike-mobility platform via Observability & Reliability Operations and Identity & Access Management.

Support the KR by contributing capability for program go-live lead time, aligned with its focus on own employer-facing launch, policy, and KPI workflows that make bike programs easier to configure and expand via Employer Policy & Program Management and Quote, Subsidy & Savings Configurator.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR is deadline-sensitive enough that the organization should manage it as a commitment rather than a stretch outcome.

target1.label: 2026-Q2 target
target1.target: Cut launch time for newly signed employers
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Program go-live lead time moves far enough to show clear momentum toward cut launch time for newly signed employers, driven by reduce the time from employer signature to enrollment-ready launch by standardizing policy setup and payroll onboarding.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target
target2.target: Cut launch time for newly signed employers
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Program go-live lead time ends the horizon sustainably lower than today's baseline, with cut launch time for newly signed employers holding across the broader operating model.

keyResults.target: Lift first-wave participation in newly launched employers
keyResults.baseline: Adoption depends heavily on launch quality and local communications
keyResults.statement: Increase adoption among eligible employees by making enrollment and bike selection easier in the first 90 days after launch.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to improve adoption among eligible employees by making enrollment and bike selection easier in the first 90 days after launch via Employer Policy & Program Management and Quote, Subsidy & Savings Configurator.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for eligible employee bike adoption, aligned with its focus on own the HRIS, payroll, SSO, and employer-system integration layer required for repeatable launches via Eligibility, Payroll & HRIS Sync and Enterprise Integration Layer.

Support the KR by contributing capability for eligible employee bike adoption, aligned with its focus on own settlement, invoicing, payroll reconciliation, and financial control across employers, riders, and partners via Settlement, Invoicing & Payroll Reconciliation and Lease Origination & Credit Decisioning.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR is deadline-sensitive enough that the organization should manage it as a commitment rather than a stretch outcome.

target1.label: 2026-Q2 target
target1.target: Lift first-wave participation in newly launched employers
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Eligible employee bike adoption moves far enough to show clear momentum toward lift first-wave participation in newly launched employers, driven by increase adoption among eligible employees by making enrollment and bike selection easier in the first 90 days after launch.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target
target2.target: Lift first-wave participation in newly launched employers
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Eligible employee bike adoption ends the horizon sustainably ahead of today's baseline, with lift first-wave participation in newly launched employers holding across the broader operating model.

keyResults.metric: Net revenue retention from employer programs
keyResults.target: Strengthen renewal and expansion economics in the active employer base
keyResults.baseline: Retention varies by service quality, adoption, and country maturity
keyResults.statement: Increase employer revenue retention by reducing operational friction and creating a clearer expansion path into additional countries and employee groups.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to improve employer revenue retention by reducing operational friction and creating a clearer expansion path into additional countries and employee groups via Employer Policy & Program Management and Quote, Subsidy & Savings Configurator.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for net revenue retention from employer programs, aligned with its focus on own the HRIS, payroll, SSO, and employer-system integration layer required for repeatable launches via Eligibility, Payroll & HRIS Sync and Enterprise Integration Layer.

Support the KR by contributing capability for net revenue retention from employer programs, aligned with its focus on own settlement, invoicing, payroll reconciliation, and financial control across employers, riders, and partners via Settlement, Invoicing & Payroll Reconciliation and Lease Origination & Credit Decisioning.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR is deadline-sensitive enough that the organization should manage it as a commitment rather than a stretch outcome.

target1.label: 2026-Q2 target
target1.target: Strengthen renewal and expansion economics in the active employer base
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Net revenue retention from employer programs moves far enough to show clear momentum toward strengthen renewal and expansion economics in the active employer base, driven by increase employer revenue retention by reducing operational friction and creating a clearer expansion path into additional countries and employee groups.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target
target2.target: Strengthen renewal and expansion economics in the active employer base
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Net revenue retention from employer programs ends the horizon sustainably ahead of today's baseline, with strengthen renewal and expansion economics in the active employer base holding across the broader operating model.

linkedInitiatives.description: Employer launch acceleration initiative focused on improving employer policy & program management, eligibility, payroll & HRIS sync, and settlement, invoicing & payroll reconciliation to make multi-country employer rollouts faster and less manual across Employer Admin Portal, Employer Integration API, and Employer KPI Cockpit.

linkedReleases.description: Release of employer rollout improvements covering employer policy & program management, eligibility, payroll & HRIS sync, and settlement, invoicing & payroll reconciliation across Employer Admin Portal, Employer Integration API, and Employer KPI Cockpit.

objectives.title: 2026-Q1 Turn interested employees into confident active riders
objectives.objective: Improve the rider journey from quote to daily commuting by making affordability clearer, order progress more transparent, and service recovery faster when problems happen.

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Share of riders who convert from configured quote to confirmed order.
Average time to restore rider mobility after a reported issue.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Count of riders with a live contract and recent activity.

keyResults.target: Convert more configured riders into confirmed orders
keyResults.baseline: Conversion drops when savings are unclear or order steps feel opaque
keyResults.statement: Increase quote-to-order conversion by clarifying monthly cost, expected savings, and dealer handover expectations before commitment.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to improve quote-to-order conversion by clarifying monthly cost, expected savings, and dealer handover expectations before commitment via Bike Catalog & Recommendation and Order Orchestration & Checkout.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for quote-to-order conversion, aligned with its focus on own native rider mobile experiences and the app surfaces that maintain confidence after checkout via Rider Identity, Wallet & App Experience.

Support the KR by contributing capability for quote-to-order conversion, aligned with its focus on own dealer onboarding, coverage, stock readiness, and handover planning via Dealer & Service Partner Network Management and Stock, Availability & Handover Planning.

Support the KR by contributing capability for quote-to-order conversion, aligned with its focus on own notifications, lifecycle state visibility, renewal readiness, and contract confidence after the initial order via Communications & Notifications and Contract Lifecycle Management.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should be pursued as an outcome target with room for discovery, iteration, and tradeoff.

target1.label: 2026-Q2 target
target1.target: Convert more configured riders into confirmed orders
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Quote-to-order conversion moves far enough to show clear momentum toward convert more configured riders into confirmed orders, driven by increase quote-to-order conversion by clarifying monthly cost, expected savings, and dealer handover expectations before commitment.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target
target2.target: Convert more configured riders into confirmed orders
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Quote-to-order conversion ends the horizon sustainably ahead of today's baseline, with convert more configured riders into confirmed orders holding across the broader operating model.

keyResults.target: Restore rider mobility faster after theft, damage, or breakdown
keyResults.baseline: Recovery speed varies by partner responsiveness and case complexity
keyResults.statement: Reduce incident recovery time by giving riders better status visibility and faster routing into service and claims workflows.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to reduce incident recovery time by giving riders better status visibility and faster routing into service and claims workflows via Communications & Notifications and Contract Lifecycle Management.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for incident recovery time, aligned with its focus on own maintenance, repair, and service workflows that determine rider uptime and partner throughput via Service Case & Maintenance Orchestration.

Support the KR by contributing capability for incident recovery time, aligned with its focus on own insurance and claims workflows so riders, dealers, and finance teams can resolve incidents with less friction via Insurance & Claims Administration.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should be pursued as an outcome target with room for discovery, iteration, and tradeoff.

target1.label: 2026-Q2 target
target1.target: Restore rider mobility faster after theft, damage, or breakdown
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Incident recovery time moves far enough to show clear momentum toward restore rider mobility faster after theft, damage, or breakdown, driven by reduce incident recovery time by giving riders better status visibility and faster routing into service and claims workflows.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target
target2.target: Restore rider mobility faster after theft, damage, or breakdown
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Incident recovery time ends the horizon sustainably lower than today's baseline, with restore rider mobility faster after theft, damage, or breakdown holding across the broader operating model.

keyResults.target: Grow the number of riders who stay active after onboarding
keyResults.baseline: Activation and early churn differ across employers and markets
keyResults.statement: Increase active riders by improving both onboarding conversion and the first-service experience after the bike is delivered.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to improve active riders by improving both onboarding conversion and the first-service experience after the bike is delivered via Bike Catalog & Recommendation and Order Orchestration & Checkout.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for active riders, aligned with its focus on own native rider mobile experiences and the app surfaces that maintain confidence after checkout via Rider Identity, Wallet & App Experience.

Support the KR by contributing capability for active riders, aligned with its focus on own dealer onboarding, coverage, stock readiness, and handover planning via Dealer & Service Partner Network Management and Stock, Availability & Handover Planning.

Support the KR by contributing capability for active riders, aligned with its focus on own notifications, lifecycle state visibility, renewal readiness, and contract confidence after the initial order via Communications & Notifications and Contract Lifecycle Management.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should be pursued as an outcome target with room for discovery, iteration, and tradeoff.

target1.label: 2026-Q2 target
target1.target: Grow the number of riders who stay active after onboarding
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Active riders moves far enough to show clear momentum toward grow the number of riders who stay active after onboarding, driven by increase active riders by improving both onboarding conversion and the first-service experience after the bike is delivered.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target
target2.target: Grow the number of riders who stay active after onboarding
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Active riders ends the horizon sustainably ahead of today's baseline, with grow the number of riders who stay active after onboarding holding across the broader operating model.

linkedInitiatives.description: Rider conversion and trust initiative focused on improving bike catalog & recommendation, order orchestration & checkout, rider identity, wallet & app experience, and communications & notifications across iOS App, Android App, Rider Web Experience, and Lifecycle Notifications.

Partner service control tower initiative focused on improving dealer & service partner network management, stock, availability & handover planning, service case & maintenance orchestration, and insurance & claims administration across Partner Portal and Partner Integration API.

linkedReleases.description: Release of rider conversion and transparency improvements covering bike catalog & recommendation, order orchestration & checkout, rider identity, wallet & app experience, and communications & notifications across iOS App, Android App, Rider Web Experience, and Lifecycle Notifications.

Release of partner service workflow improvements covering dealer & service partner network management, stock, availability & handover planning, service case & maintenance orchestration, and insurance & claims administration across Partner Portal and Partner Integration API.
